command: [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol=[Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12; $ts=Get-Date -Format o; try { $b=[Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("$env:DEHASHED_EMAIL`:$env:DEHASHED_KEY")); $headers=@{ 'Authorization'="Basic $b"; 'Accept'='application/json' }; $r=Invoke-WebRequest -Uri 'https://api.dehashed.com/search?query=domain:contoso.com&size=50' -Headers $headers -Method Get -ErrorAction Stop; ($r.Content | ConvertFrom-Json | Select-Object -ExpandProperty entries | Select-Object email,username,password,ip_address,obtained_from | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6) | Out-File -FilePath "$env:TEMP\dehashed_contoso_leaks.json" -Encoding utf8; Add-Content -Path "$env:TEMP\recon.log" -Value "$ts Dehashed success contoso.com" } catch { Add-Content -Path "$env:TEMP\recon.log" -Value "$ts Dehashed error: $($_.Exception.Message)" }
explanation: Queries Dehashed (subscription-based breach database) with Basic auth for entries tied to the victim domain, selecting only high-value fields and logging execution.
rationale_steps: Authenticate with closed-source breach index using secure headers.
Search for compromised credentials associated with the domain.
Minimize noise by selecting specific fields and save as JSON.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes/braces, valid type/property references, proper try/catch, and correct pipelines and headers.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It authenticates to the Dehashed paid breach database, queries for a target domain, extracts relevant fields, and saves/logs results, directly aligning with T1597 Search Closed Sources.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quotes, braces, and parentheses, proper semicolon separators, valid cmdlet syntax, and correct try/catch structure throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1597 by authenticating to the Dehashed paid breach database API using Basic auth with environment-variable credentials, querying for leaked records tied to a specific victim domain, extracting high-value fields (email, username, password, IP), and logging results — fully consistent with querying closed/subscription data sources for targeting intelligence.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed, using valid syntax for setting TLS protocols, handling environment variables, performing API requests, and managing JSON data with proper error handling.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the T1597 technique by using valid API parameters and authentication methods to query Dehashed, a prominent closed-source breach database, for target intelligence.
